perturbation.original_text: Party B shall purchase sufficient insurance for the transportation vehicles. The coverage of third-party liability insurance shall not be lower than RMB1 million. In addition to vehicle personnel insurance, Party B shall at least purchase injury insurance for two persons with coverage not lower than RMB500,000 per person.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall purchase insurance for the transportation vehicles. In addition to vehicle personnel insurance, Party B may purchase injury insurance for persons.
perturbation.explanation: This modification removes the mandatory minimum insurance coverage for third-party liability and injury insurance, which is a legally required protection in many jurisdictions to ensure adequate compensation in case of accidents. By making the injury insurance optional and removing specific coverage amounts, the contract creates a legal risk.
perturbation.law_citation: Tort Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 16
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 16 of the Tort Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates that where a tort causes personal injury, expenses such as medical expenses, nursing expenses, transportation expenses, and loss of income shall be compensated. By removing the mandatory minimum insurance coverage, the modified text contradicts this law as it may leave injured parties without sufficient recourse for compensation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: ret prévoit par ailleurs qu’à compter de la disparition de la Chambre nationale des avoués le patrimoine de celle-ci est transféré au Conseil national des barreaux. Enfin, le décret précise les nouvelles modalités de la procédure permettant l’obtention de l’honorariat par les anciens avoués. Références : le décret est pris pour l’application de l’article 29 de la loi no 2011-94 du 25 janvier 2011 portant réforme de la représentation devant les cours d’appel. Les textes et les codes modifiés par le présent décret peuvent être consultés, dans leur rédaction issue de cette modification, sur le site Légifrance (http://www.legifrance.gouv.fr). Vu la loi no 71-1130 du 31 décembre 1971 modifiée portant réforme de certaines professions judiciaires et juridiques, notamment son article 1er ; Vu la loi no 2011-94 du 25 janvier 2011 portant réforme de la représentation devant les cours d’appel, notamment ses articles 25 et 27 à 29 ; Vu le décret no 45-0118 du 19 décembre 1945 modifié pris pour l’application du statut des avoués ; Vu le décret no 55-22 du 4 janvier 1955 modifié portant réforme de la publicité foncière, notamment ses articles 5, 32 et 34 ; Vu le décret no 55-1350 du 14 octobre 1955 modifié pris pour l’application du décret no 55-22 du 4 janvier 1955 portant réforme de la publicité foncière, notamment ses articles 22, 23, 38, 47 et 60 ; Vu le décret no 56-221 du 29 février 1956 modifié pris pour l’application du décret no 55-604 du 20 mai 1955 relatif aux officiers publics ou ministériels et à certains auxiliaires de justice en ce qui concerne la suppléance des officiers publics et ministériels, notamment ses articles 1er, 7 et 16 ; Vu le décret no 56-222 du 29 février 1956 modifié pris pour l’application de l’ordonnance du 2 novembre 1945 relative au statut des huissiers de justice, notamment son article 11 ; Vu le décret no 58-1284 du 22 décembre 1958 portant application de l’ordonnance no 58-1273 du 22 décembre 1958 et relatif à la compétence du tribunal d’instance, du tribunal de grande instance et de la cour d’appel en matière civile ainsi qu’à la représentation et à l’assistance des parties devant ces juridictions en cette même matière, notamment son article 37 ; Vu le décret no 59-327 du 20 février 1959 mod
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates specific minimum insurance coverage for third-party liability and injury, ensuring financial protection for potential victims. The changed text removes these mandatory minimums, making injury insurance optional and removing specific coverage amounts. The Law Explanation states this contradicts Article 16 of the Tort Law of the People's Republic of China, as it may leave injured parties without sufficient compensation, which the original contract aimed to prevent. Scraped Law Snippet 1, while not directly applicable as it is French law, demonstrates the detailed and prescriptive nature of legal texts, highlighting how the removal of specific requirements from the original contract creates a legally questionable situation, as the amended contract now fails to ensure the legally required minimum protection.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising out of the execution of this Agreement, which cannot be negotiated and settled by both Parties, shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the People's Court where this Agreement is signed.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of the execution of this Agreement shall be subject to arbitration determined by Party A.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the jurisdiction of the People's Court and mandating arbitration decided solely by Party A, the contract potentially violates laws ensuring fair and impartial dispute resolution. This creates an imbalance of power and contradicts requirements for neutral arbitration processes.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 7
perturbation.law_citation: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 7
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 7 of the Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China requires that arbitration shall be conducted based on the principles of independence, impartiality, and fairness. By granting Party A the sole right to determine the arbitration process, the modified text contradicts this law as it undermines the impartiality and fairness of the dispute resolution process.
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the principle of impartiality in dispute resolution, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. By granting Party A the sole right to determine the arbitration process, it removes the People's Court jurisdiction and creates a clear imbalance, violating the requirement for a neutral process. The Omission of an independent arbiter makes the contract non-compliant.

perturbation.original_text: Party B shall provide copies of driver licenses, working licenses, occupation licenses, insurance documents, operation licenses, outsourcing contracts, tax certificates, business licenses, road transportation permits and business code
perturbation.changed_text: Party B may provide some documents to Party A.
perturbation.explanation: This modification eliminates the requirement for Party B to provide specific licenses and permits, which are legally mandated for road transportation services. This omission could expose Party A to liability if Party B operates without the necessary legal authorizations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Road Traffic Safety Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 8
perturbation.law_citation: Road Traffic Safety Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 8
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 8 of the Road Traffic Safety Law of the People's Republic of China requires drivers to obtain a driver's license in accordance with law before being permitted to drive a motor vehicle on a road. By allowing for potential non-compliance of this requirement, the modified text contradicts this law, as the original text was ensuring compliance by mandating the presence and validation of this document.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes "shall provide" to "may provide some documents," which eliminates the original requirement for Party B to provide essential documents such as driver's licenses and operating licenses. This constitutes an Omission Legal Contradiction because it removes essential information, creating legal loopholes, in particular regarding the necessity of a driver's license. The Law Explanation highlights that Article 8 of the Road Traffic Safety Law of the People's Republic of China requires drivers to have a license. The modification weakens compliance with this law, as it no longer mandates the provision and validation of necessary documentation, thus potentially exposing Party A to liability. Since the contract omits a legally required action, this change directly contradicts established legal safety standards. Since there are no scraped law snippets, the justification is based on the perturbation type, law explanation, original, and changed texts.
